meta.titleDefault: MCP Radar — Find MCP servers that work, and see which ones are still alive
meta.tagline: Find MCP servers that actually work — sorted by use case, flagged by whether they're still maintained.
meta.description: A use-case directory of MCP servers. Every server is flagged by maintenance status: active / stale / abandoned. Data from the official registry, GitHub and npm, updated daily.

footer.copyright: © 2026 MCP Radar · Scores come from public data only; rankings are never for sale

home.heroSub: {total} MCP servers indexed, each flagged by maintenance status: 🟢 active / 🟡 stale / ⚰️ abandoned.
home.categoriesSub: Sorted by "what it helps me do", not by tech stack.
home.weeklySub: The engine scans the whole ecosystem weekly — changes at a glance.
home.weeklyDead: ⚰️ Flagged abandoned / at risk this week
home.hotSub: Ranked by TrustScore, all actively maintained.
home.whyTitle: Other directories just list tools. We also tell you whether anyone still maintains it.
home.whyBody: Each server's health is computed daily by the engine: recent commits, issue response, download trend, whether the repo is archived. The scoring methodology is fully public and rankings are never for sale.
home.intentSub: Jump straight to servers that work, by what you want to solve

intents.label: scrape the web / drive a browser

sponsor.metaTitle: Sponsor — rankings are never for sale, placements are transparently priced
sponsor.metaDesc: MCP Radar's sponsorship rules: scores and rankings come from public data only; sponsorship buys clearly labeled placements only.
sponsor.integrity: Our rankings and scores come from public data only. Sponsorship only buys clearly labeled placements on detail pages, and never affects any server's TrustScore or leaderboard position.
sponsor.audienceBody: MCP Radar's readers are developers and tech leads choosing MCP servers for their projects — exactly the people who decide on dev tools, cloud services and AI products.
sponsor.audienceNote: (Subscriber and traffic figures will be published once we have volume.)
sponsor.claimTitle: Are you a server author? Claim your detail page
sponsor.claimBody: Once claimed, you can add an official description, docs links, support channels and screenshots, and the page shows an "Officially claimed" badge — none of which affects TrustScore or ranking. It just makes your listing more complete and trustworthy.
sponsor.claimFree2: ✓ Edit official description / docs / support channels
sponsor.claimFreeNote: Enabled after email verification of repo ownership
sponsor.rush1: ✓ New server reviewed and listed within 3 business days
sponsor.rushNote: Still must meet the public-data listing criteria
sponsor.slotsSub: For vendors who want to reach developers making a choice. Every placement is clearly labeled "Sponsored" and fully isolated from scores and rankings.
sponsor.slotNewsletterDesc: One clearly labeled "Sponsored" recommendation in the newsletter, reaching subscribed developers.
sponsor.slotDetailDesc: A sidebar placement on same-category detail pages, labeled "Sponsored", with no effect on that page's server scores.
sponsor.slotGuideDesc: Named sponsorship of an in-depth selection guide; editorial independence stays with us.
sponsor.slotsNote: Prices above are indicative; final quotes depend on traffic and engagement term.
sponsor.contactBody: To sponsor or request a media kit, email:

verdict.dead: ⛔ Repo archived, author stopped maintaining — don't install
verdict.unverifiable: ⚪ Remote-only, no open-source repo, behavior can't be audited — grant permissions cautiously

deathReason.stale: Last commit {days} days ago, weak issue response, no new release

graveyard.metaTitle: MCP Graveyard — these servers are dead, stop installing them
graveyard.metaDesc: The list of abandoned and at-risk MCP servers: cause of death, date flagged, past stars. Check before you install, don't plant a landmine in your project.
graveyard.h1: ⚰️ MCP Graveyard — these servers are dead, stop installing them
graveyard.subB: dead / at-risk servers. They're still recommended in every "awesome MCP" list, but the repos have long been unmaintained. Check before you install.
graveyard.appeal: Maintainer thinks the call is wrong? Our manual review channel is always open —

about.metaTitle: About — the scoring methodology, fully public
about.metaDesc: MCP Radar's TrustScore weights, data sources, death-call rules and update frequency are fully public, no black box. We also flag boundaries like unverifiable remote-only servers.
about.intro: Because the scoring rules, data sources and decision logic are all written on this page, and every server page notes its data sources and fetch time. No black box — you can re-check any score with the same public data.
about.ownerBody: MCP Radar is maintained by an independent developer who has long tracked the AI tooling ecosystem. The reason for building this is simple: existing MCP directories only tell you "this tool exists", nobody tells you "whether it's still alive" — you install an abandoned server and debug for hours before realizing the problem isn't yours. That judgment can be fully automated, so we did it, and made it fully public.
about.ownerSignNote: (brand byline, real name can be swapped in later)
about.methodIntro: TrustScore is out of 100, weighted across five dimensions, all signals from public APIs:
about.wMaintenanceSig: Days since last commit, commits in 90 days, average issue response, whether archived
about.wAdoptionSig: GitHub stars and trend (weighted above npm downloads), npm weekly download trend, release frequency
about.wUsabilitySig: In official registry, server.json spec, whether a runnable entry can be resolved
about.wHealthSig: Open issue/PR backlog ratio, whether license is clear
about.wCommunitySig: Contributor count, fork activity, awesome-list inclusions
about.ruleDying: Last commit > 180 days AND no issue response AND no new release
about.ruleUnverifiable: Remote-only (no open-source repo / no package), unverifiable
about.lifecycleNote: "Has an auditable repo" is a usability bonus — a remote-only service can't have its behavior verified, which is itself a risk signal.
about.srcRegistryFields: Manifest, repo url, package name, official status, publish/update time
about.srcGithubFields: Last commit, commits in 90 days, issue response, archived, stars, forks, open issues, license, contributors
about.srcNpmNote: Underestimates servers installed directly from GitHub
about.updateFreq: Update frequency: health data is scanned incrementally daily, with a full diff weekly (the radar page's data source).
about.boundaryBody: remote-only servers have no repo signals and are always marked unverifiable; npm downloads underestimate servers installed directly from GitHub, so stars are weighted higher; fetching has lag, so a project that just resumed maintenance may still be briefly marked dying — which is exactly why we keep a correction channel open.
about.disclosureBody1: This site accepts sponsored detail-page placements (clearly labeled "Sponsored"), but
about.disclosureBold: rankings, scores and categories never accept bidding of any kind
about.disclosureBody2: . Sponsorship buys placement, not a score. The moment we accept ranking bids, the credibility of our proprietary data collapses — that's the foundation of this site, not for sale.
about.correctionBody: Maintainer thinks a server's score or call is wrong (e.g. maintenance resumed, repo moved)? Send the repo URL and details; we review manually and update within 48 hours:

category.descTpl: {description} {count} listed, each flagged by maintenance status and health data.

guides.metaTitle: Guides — in-depth MCP selection and rollout
guides.metaDesc: MCP server due-diligence checklists, security red lines, self-host decisions, production checklists. Free guides build understanding, member guides give templates and checklists.
guides.sub: Not rehashed tutorials — judgments reverse-engineered from site-wide health data. Free guides build understanding, member guides give you ready-to-use checklists and templates.
guides.notTranslated: An English version of this guide isn't ready yet — showing the original Chinese.
